sections.heading: Overview
sections.paragraph: Recent industry developments highlight strategic expansions in specialized manufacturing capabilities relevant to proteolysis targeting chimeras (PROTACs). Ofichem, a Netherlands-based contract development and manufacturing organization, announced a unification of its business units under a single trading name effective April 1, 2026, to streamline engagement for pharmaceutical and biotechnology customers. This reorganization consolidates active pharmaceutical ingredient, drug product, and supply capabilities into an integrated offering. Crucially for PROTAC development, Ofichem's 2025 acquisition of a site in Uppsala, Sweden, from Meribel Pharma Solutions expanded non-GMP capabilities for early-phase small-molecule API development. The facility specifically added expertise in modalities including antibody-drug conjugates, oligonucleotides, and proteolysis targeting chimeras. Additionally, Ofichem acquired Avivia in 2025 to enhance pharmaceutical development services with formulation expertise. These moves strengthen Ofichem's position as a European CDMO partner focused on supply chain reliability and regulatory expertise, providing end-to-end support from early development through commercial manufacturing. The unified structure offers greater visibility into capabilities in API development and drug substance manufacturing while maintaining quality standards. This consolidation supports the growing demand for specialized modalities like PROTACs within the contract manufacturing landscape, ensuring clients have access to integrated partners capable of handling complex small-molecule and targeted degradation technologies throughout the drug lifecycle.

points.text: Ofichem — PROTACs — Early-phase API development — Expanded non-GMP capabilities and added expertise via Uppsala site acquisition
Ofichem: Unifying business units under a single trading name to simplify engagement and strengthen its position as a European CDMO partner with expanded PROTAC manufacturing capabilities.
PROTACs — Small-molecule degradation modality — Early-phase API development — Added expertise via Uppsala facility acquisition for non-GMP capabilities.
Ofichem acquired a site in Uppsala, Sweden from Meribel Pharma Solutions in 2025 to expand PROTAC and small-molecule API capabilities.
Ofichem acquired Avivia in 2025 to expand pharmaceutical development services and formulation expertise.
